statusDate: Updated continuously via amending Acts (most recently Energy Act 2023)
summary: The foundational statute for Great Britain's electricity industry. Establishes the licensing regime (generation, transmission, distribution, supply, ISOP), creates GEMA/Ofgem's powers, defines the Secretary of State's functions, and sets out duties on licence holders. All of the industry codes below exist because licences issued under this Act require licensees to be party to them.
scope: Licensing: generation, transmission, distribution, supply, interconnector, smart meter, ISOP licences
Creation and functions of GEMA (Ofgem's board)
Powers of the Secretary of State (consent for overhead lines, generating stations s.36, compulsory purchase)
Standard licence conditions and licence modification procedures
Connection obligations (s.16: duty to connect on request) and charges
Special administration regime for failing licensees

keyParts.name: Part I — Reorganisation of the Electricity Industry
keyParts.detail: Licensing regime, the duties of the Secretary of State and GEMA, licensable activities, standard conditions, modifications.

keyParts.name: Part II — Miscellaneous and Supplemental
keyParts.detail: Consents for overhead lines, wayleaves, compulsory purchase, meters, electrical safety.

keyParts.name: Schedule 3 — Compulsory acquisition of land by licence holders
keyParts.detail: Used by TOs/DNOs to acquire rights for new substations and lines.

keyParts.name: Schedule 4 — Other powers of licence holders
keyParts.detail: Street works, wayleaves, felling and lopping trees.

relationships.amendedBy: Utilities Act 2000, Energy Act 2004, Energy Act 2013, Energy Act 2023 (created ISOP licence → NESO)

notableSections.name: Principal objective and general duties of the Secretary of State and GEMA
notableSections.detail: Protect the interests of consumers, including carbon targets and security of supply.

notableSections.name: General duties of licence holders
notableSections.detail: Duties to develop and maintain an efficient, coordinated and economical system.

notableSections.name: Duty to connect on request
notableSections.detail: Core basis of distribution connections — the reason DNOs must accept applications.

notableSections.name: Consent for construction etc. of generating stations
notableSections.detail: Required for generating stations above specified thresholds (onshore >50 MW, offshore routed via DCO under Planning Act 2008).
